“Here are the screenshots I uploaded.
Please design the SimplePrep application using these screenshots only as visual references for style, layout, and flow.
Do NOT use any links—only use the images.”

Design requirements:

1. Home (Landing Page)

Design a landing page inspired by the uploaded screenshots.

Include a navigation bar: Home | Practice | Recordings

Large centralized button: Start Practicing

Clean, modern layout similar to reference images.

2. Practice Page

Use screenshot-inspired layout for the category selection area.

When a user selects a category, show the questions on the same page.

Display 2 question cards per row (instead of 3).

3. Question Page

Follow the interaction style shown in screenshots.

Include:

Camera view under the question

Input options: Voice, Camera, Typing, AI (lightbulb icon)

If AI is selected → show AI-suggested answer in a card

User answer transcription section

Replace “Copy answer” with Save

Add Replay next to Redo

4. Recordings Page

A page showing saved video/voice recordings and saved answers.

**Refining Requirements Gathering**

I'm currently focused on the initial analysis. The SimplePrep project goal is clear: a React app based on screenshots and design specifications.  I'm identifying the tech stack and project scope. I'm prioritizing understanding all the design requirements. My next step will be to create a detailed list of features.


**Structuring the Components**

I've been drilling down into the technical details and feel I now have a solid understanding of the component breakdown. The file structure will be XML formatted, starting with `metadata.json`, `index.tsx`, `index.html`, and `App.tsx`, followed by components and services. The core layout will be handled within `App.tsx` and the nav bar will be a sticky component.


**Detailing Component Architecture**

I'm now diving into the component breakdown and have settled on the files needed for SimplePrep. I will use the established layout file structure to organize each component and service file. The logic will involve `Home.tsx` as the landing page, `Practice.tsx` will include category selection, and the main interaction will be in `QuestionModal.tsx`. The saved answer feature will be represented in `Recordings.tsx`. Finally, AI logic is encapsulated in `geminiService.ts`. I also made sure to create `types.ts` for interfaces.


**Mapping Question Interactions**

I'm now focusing on the intricate question interactions.  I've decided to implement a modal interface, complete with a simulated recording feature and a `SpeechRecognition` implementation for voice input, enhancing its responsiveness. The AI will provide insights, and recordings will be saved using the local storage API. Also, I'll be utilizing Tailwind's styling for UI components.


**Implementing Core Functionality**

I'm now writing the core components, starting with a mental draft that's translating directly into code, with metadata, types, and the main application structure outlined. `Home.tsx` and `Practice.tsx` are taking shape, incorporating key UI elements. The `QuestionInteraction.tsx` component is where the interaction lives, including webcam and voice inputs. I've also integrated the `geminiService.ts` for AI-powered responses. I've chosen `gemini-2.5-flash` to optimize speed. Finally, I've acknowledged the potential for incompatibility with the Web Speech API and have added a fallback for environments where it's not supported.


**Adding API Failback**

I'm expanding my work with a "Simulate" mode as a fallback if the SpeechRecognition API fails, ensuring broader compatibility. Addressing a camera permissions issue, I'll add clear buttons for access. Following the requirements, I'm adjusting layouts for two question cards per row and placing the camera view appropriately. I've also finalized the core component and service file structure. Tailwind styling is set up with backgrounds, card styles, and button colors for a clean interface.


**Crafting XML Components**

I'm now generating the XML string blocks. I realized a slight misinterpretation on the `QuestionModal` interface, so I'm implementing input toggles for voice, camera, and typing, and the AI-suggested answer will appear when the AI option is selected. I am also planning to refactor to keep `saved recordings` state in App.tsx. I've double checked for constraints. Let's start.

I'm now focusing on redesigning the SimplePrep application, considering new requirements and visual cues. My primary concern is incorporating the specified aesthetic into a fresh and intuitive user experience. The challenge lies in balancing the functionality with the desired look and feel.

I am in the midst of implementing the new card style, including the subtle shadow effect on hover and persistent shadow on click, ensuring a seamless user experience.


**Evaluating Button Design**

I'm now focusing on button styles, considering the white background and specified opacities. The existing context raises questions about visual clarity, especially with white buttons on a white page. I'm considering text-only buttons for elements like "See all the questions" to maintain readability and aesthetic consistency, given the light gray background. Navigation logic is also being reviewed, particularly the "Start" action's link to the Voice Option Flow.


**Developing Question Flow**

I'm now refining the progression through the question screens. I've designed the **All Questions Page**, envisioning a grid layout for efficient browsing. Upon entry, the system will immediately read the question aloud via TTS. Clicking a question will lead to the **Voice Option Flow**, where I've detailed initial and voice-specific states, including automatic speech-to-text conversion. I'm focusing on "Redo" functionality now.


**Defining Redo Behavior**

Clicking "Yes" reverts to the question reading state. If the "Don't ask again" option is checked, I'll implement a mechanism to remember the user's preference.

I'm now implementing the camera feature: a camera preview displays with a "Start" button. Pressing "Start" enables recording and transforms the button into "Done." Clicking "Done" will take us to the transcript review and redo options screen. This leads me to further changes within the file structure.

The `types.ts` file will require updating the `View` and `QuestionCategory` enumerations, the Home view has been removed since we no longer have a landing page.


**Structuring File Organization**

I'm now focusing on a more efficient file structure. I'm eliminating the Home screen entirely, opting for FieldSelection as the new entry point. The existing Practice component will become a comprehensive QuestionList.  I'm also streamlining the QuestionModal component to serve as a versatile QuestionFlow, incorporating a complex state machine for the question-answering process. My immediate priority is to integrate a robust state management system to ensure smooth transitions between the Reading, Input Selection, Recording, Review, and Redo Confirmation states.


**Sequencing the Question Flow**

I'm currently working to streamline the user journey through the new question flow. I've defined the key states: `PREVIEW_CAMERA`, `RECORDING_CAMERA`, `TYPING`, `REVIEW`, and `REDO_CONFIRM`. The "Voice" and "Camera" options will directly influence the starting state. I'm focusing on ensuring seamless transitions between these states, including proper handling of the back and forward arrow functionality. The logic behind transitioning between screens is now being implemented.


**Removing Unused Components**

I'm presently removing unused files, specifically `Home.tsx` and `Recordings.tsx`, as they are no longer required based on the most recent design specifications. I'm prioritizing strict adherence to the new prompt's requirements, focusing solely on the specified screens and eliminating unnecessary components. I'm now adjusting the code to reflect the new navigation, treating "Screen 3" as a full-page view rather than a modal.


**Implementing Components**

I'm now coding the new components, starting with `FieldSelection`. I've implemented the grid layout for categories, handling state changes on selection, and the corresponding "Start" and "See All" actions. I've also incorporated the navigation logic based on the user's choices, ensuring a smooth transition to the appropriate screens. I'm focusing on ensuring visual consistency, particularly with the white background and button styles. The next step will be to implement the All Questions screen, which will retrieve the questions based on the selected category.


**Defining Screen Navigation**

I'm now focusing on navigation logic and state management across screens. I've integrated a basic history stack in `App.tsx` for the "Back" arrow functionality, handling transitions between states. The "Forward" arrow will advance to the next question in the `QuestionFlow` component. I'm prioritizing the implementation of a full-screen `QuestionFlow`, and making sure I have the necessary states available to use.


**Updating Type Definitions**

I'm now integrating type definitions into the codebase. I am establishing the data types and structures needed for managing the application's state and data flow. I'm focusing on creating robust and extensible types within the `types.ts` file. These types will ensure the proper structure and consistency for interactions, states, and data throughout the application's lifecycle.


**Building Out Screens**

I'm now focused on implementing the screen components, starting with the navigation bar, which I've simplified to only have back and forward arrows. I'm moving on to building out the field selection and all questions screens. I'm focusing on getting these components implemented, and building the flow. I need to ensure that the proper screen is shown based on previous selections.

Based on your detailed requirements and the visual flow described, I will redesign the SimplePrep application. I will transition the app from the previous landing page structure to the new flow: **Field Selection -> All Questions -> Question Flow**.

Here are the changes:

1.  **Global UI**: Clean white background, simplified navigation with only Back/Forward arrows.
2.  **Field Selection (Screen 1)**: A grid of category cards that reveals a "Start" section upon selection.
3.  **All Questions (Screen 2)**: A 2-column grid layout for browsing questions within a category.
4.  **Question Flow (Screen 3)**: A dedicated immersive view for answering, featuring auto-TTS, Voice/Camera/Typing modes, and a specific "Redo" workflow with confirmation.

**Reviewing UI Updates**

I'm currently focused on the proposed UI modifications for the SimplePrep application, specifically the global changes. I am considering the impact of the requested page background color adjustments. I am also working through the design suggestions, and I need to ensure that these changes are integrated seamlessly into the existing app structure without causing conflicts.


**Integrating New Design Elements**

I've been meticulously integrating the new design elements. I've tackled the color scheme, settling on `#ECF0F6` as the background. White content containers are implemented and I've also incorporated the navigation bar's updated icon design, now inside square containers with rounded corners, and I am finalizing the hover sound feedback implementation.  I'm progressing through the landing page refinements, specifically the conditional display of the "Practice with 5" section based on category selection. I've also just completed the Custom field logic: I'm directing the user to the new custom field view with the text box and the start button. I'm now transitioning into the manual question process design.


**Refining Tag Interactions**

I'm focused on the 'i' icon implementation for question tags. It needs to be visually distinct, so I'm researching the best approach for the UI, considering both the icon itself and its placement within the existing design. Additionally, I need to ensure the correct functionality is linked with these icons. The recording transcription feature is also under review.


**Designing Hover Feedback**

I'm currently creating the hover sound feedback implementation.  My plan is to create a `useSound` hook, which will use either a base64 encoded "tin" sound or a silent fallback. I'll utilize the `Audio` constructor with a placeholder sound URL. The code is in-progress.


**Finalizing UI Features**

I'm now fully immersed in the implementation of the remaining UI features. I've completed the view rendering for `CUSTOM_JOB_DESCRIPTION` and `ADD_CUSTOM_QUESTION`. State management for custom questions and job descriptions is also implemented. The `FieldSelection` component has been updated; I've set the background to transparent or matching `#ECF0F6`, made the cards white, and added the custom section logic. I've created the new components `CustomJobDescription.tsx` and `AddCustomQuestion.tsx`, and I've also added the information icon to the question tags in the `Practice` component. Lastly, I've embedded a tiny base64 sound for the "tin" effect to ensure it works immediately.


**Designing Custom Interactions**

I'm currently fleshing out the custom question flow. I've designed the route from the home screen's "Custom field..." option to the `View.CUSTOM_JOB_DESCRIPTION` and am including the textarea and "Start" button. I'm also planning the flow to `View.ADD_CUSTOM_QUESTION`, including the input for the question and the "Your answer" section. I'm now clarifying the relationship between the question input and the preview within the card, and am also confirming the screenshot details.


**Defining Custom Input Flow**

I'm now implementing the custom question flow. I've designed the route from the home screen's "Custom field..." option to the `View.CUSTOM_JOB_DESCRIPTION` with the expected textarea and "Start" button. I'm also planning the flow to `View.ADD_CUSTOM_QUESTION`, including the input for the question and the "Your answer" section. I'm focusing on clarifying the relationship between the question input and the preview within the card, along with confirming the screenshot details. I have identified the need for an input field for the custom question text and an "Add" button to add it to the session and start practice or add it to the list. Now, I'm working through the codebase to update `index.html`, `types.ts`, `Navbar.tsx`, `FieldSelection.tsx`, and the new components `CustomJobDescription.tsx`, `AddCustomQuestion.tsx`, and `Practice.tsx` in that order. I'll also add a hover sound implementation.


**Planning Sound Implementation**

I'm now focusing on integrating the audio feedback. I'll create a `useSound` hook for reusability, aiming for a base64 encoded "tin" sound or a silent fallback. I'll set up `utils/audio.ts` with a `playHover` function.  I've updated `index.html` with a background-color, applied button styles to the navbar, and tweaked `FieldSelection.tsx`. I've added custom field selection logic to `App.tsx`.


**Finalizing Custom Question Display**

I'm now focusing on the custom question display. I've confirmed that the "Your answer" section is integrated into the card once a custom question is added. I'm implementing the `MANUAL_ADD` view, the textarea, and the "Start" button as per the design.  I've added the input for the custom question and the "Add" button for the addition to the session or list. I am prioritizing a placeholder sound implementation until I can source a valid base64 audio.


**Confirming Input Functionality**

I'm now clarifying the custom input flow. I'm focusing on the "Type your question here" input and confirming its role. The design specifies an "Add" button, suggesting the text area is for question input only. The "Your answer" section is visual, likely for preview. I'll make the "Type your question here" an input and the "Your answer" section a static element, as per the screenshot. I'm building it now.

To implement the design updates and new features, I will update the application structure to support the new Custom Question flows, refresh the global UI styling to match the visual design system, and implement the interactive sound feedback.

Here is the plan:
1.  **Global Styles**: Update the background color in `index.html`.
2.  **Navigation**: Redesign the Navbar icons to be square with 25% rounding.
3.  **App Logic**: Add new views for `CUSTOM_DESCRIPTION` and `CUSTOM_ADD` in `App.tsx` and `types.ts`.
4.  **Field Selection**: Update the Landing Page to handle the specific behavior for "Custom" field selection (navigating away) vs other fields (expanding inline), and apply new spacing/shadows.
5.  **New Components**: Create `CustomJobInput.tsx` for the job description entry and `CustomQuestionInput.tsx` for manually adding questions.
6.  **Practice Page**: Add the info icon to tags in `Practice.tsx`.
7.  **Sound**: Implement a subtle hover sound utility.

export default function App() {
  const [history, setHistory] = useState<View[]>([View.FIELD_SELECTION]);
  const [currentViewIndex, setCurrentViewIndex] = useState(0);
  
  const [selectedCategory, setSelectedCategory] = useState<QuestionCategory | null>(null);
  const [selectedQuestion, setSelectedQuestion] = useState<Question | null>(null);
  const [questions, setQuestions] = useState(INITIAL_QUESTIONS);
  const [dontAskRedo, setDontAskRedo] = useState(false);

  const currentView = history[currentViewIndex];

  const navigateTo = (view: View) => {
    const newHistory = history.slice(0, currentViewIndex + 1);
    newHistory.push(view);
    setHistory(newHistory);
    setCurrentViewIndex(newHistory.length - 1);
  };

  const handleBack = () => {
    if (currentViewIndex > 0) {
      setCurrentViewIndex(prev => prev - 1);
    }
  };

  const handleForward = () => {
    if (currentViewIndex < history.length - 1) {
      setCurrentViewIndex(prev => prev + 1);
    }
  };

  const handleSelectCategory = (cat: QuestionCategory) => {
    setSelectedCategory(cat);
    if (cat === QuestionCategory.CUSTOM) {
      navigateTo(View.CUSTOM_DESCRIPTION);
    }
  };

  const handleStartPractice = () => {
    // Get all available questions for the selected category from the initial bank
    const allQuestions = INITIAL_QUESTIONS[selectedCategory!] || [];

    // Shuffle and pick 5 random questions
    const shuffled = [...allQuestions].sort(() => 0.5 - Math.random());
    const randomFive = shuffled.slice(0, 5);

    // Update the state to only show these 5 for the session
    setQuestions(prev => ({
      ...prev,
      [selectedCategory!]: randomFive
    }));

    if (randomFive.length > 0) {
      setSelectedQuestion(randomFive[0]);
      navigateTo(View.QUESTION_FLOW);
    } else {
       // Fallback or empty state
       navigateTo(View.ALL_QUESTIONS);
    }
  };

  const handleStartCustomJob = (description: string, generatedQuestions: string[]) => {
    // Convert generated strings to Question objects
    const newQuestions: Question[] = generatedQuestions.map((text, index) => ({
      id: `gen-${Date.now()}-${index}`,
      text: text,
      category: QuestionCategory.CUSTOM,
      type: 'Custom question'
    }));

    setQuestions(prev => ({
      ...prev,
      [QuestionCategory.CUSTOM]: [...(prev[QuestionCategory.CUSTOM] || []), ...newQuestions]
    }));
    
    // As per requirement "Display the list of questions that were generated earlier",
    // when "See all generated questions" is clicked in CustomJobInput, we navigate here.
    setSelectedCategory(QuestionCategory.CUSTOM);
    navigateTo(View.ALL_QUESTIONS);
  };

  const handleManualAddCustom = () => {
    navigateTo(View.CUSTOM_ADD);
  };

  const handleAddCustomQuestion = (text: string) => {
    const newQuestion: Question = {
      id: Date.now().toString(),
      text: text,
      category: QuestionCategory.CUSTOM,
      type: 'Custom question'
    };
    
    setQuestions(prev => {
      const updated = {
          ...prev,
          [QuestionCategory.CUSTOM]: [...(prev[QuestionCategory.CUSTOM] || []), newQuestion]
      };
      return updated;
    });

    setSelectedCategory(QuestionCategory.CUSTOM); 
    navigateTo(View.ALL_QUESTIONS);
  };

  const handleSeeAllQuestions = () => {
    // If the user wants to see all questions, we should ensure the full list is loaded
    // instead of the potential subset from "Practice with 5"
    if (selectedCategory && selectedCategory !== QuestionCategory.CUSTOM) {
       setQuestions(prev => ({
          ...prev,
          [selectedCategory]: INITIAL_QUESTIONS[selectedCategory]
       }));
    }
    navigateTo(View.ALL_QUESTIONS);
  };

  const handleSelectQuestion = (q: Question) => {
    setSelectedQuestion(q);
    navigateTo(View.QUESTION_FLOW);
  };

  const renderView = () => {
    switch (currentView) {
      case View.FIELD_SELECTION:
        return (
          <FieldSelection 
            selectedCategory={selectedCategory}
            onSelectCategory={handleSelectCategory}
            onStartPractice={handleStartPractice}
            onSeeAllQuestions={handleSeeAllQuestions}
          />
        );
      case View.CUSTOM_DESCRIPTION:
        return (
          <CustomJobInput 
            onStart={handleStartCustomJob}
            onManualAdd={handleManualAddCustom}
          />
        );
      case View.CUSTOM_ADD:
        return (
          <CustomQuestionInput 
             onAdd={handleAddCustomQuestion}
          />
        );
      case View.ALL_QUESTIONS:
        return (
          <Practice 
            category={selectedCategory || QuestionCategory.UX_DESIGN}
            questions={questions[selectedCategory!] || questions[QuestionCategory.UX_DESIGN]}
            onSelectQuestion={handleSelectQuestion}
          />
        );
      case View.QUESTION_FLOW:
        return (
          <QuestionFlow 
            question={selectedQuestion!}
            onComplete={(rec) => console.log(rec)}
            dontAskRedo={dontAskRedo}
            setDontAskRedo={setDontAskRedo}
          />
        );
      default:
        return <div>Unknown View</div>;
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen text-slate-900 flex flex-col">
      <Navbar 
        onBack={handleBack} 
        onForward={handleForward}
        canGoBack={currentViewIndex > 0}
        canGoForward={currentViewIndex < history.length - 1}
      />
      <main className="flex-grow">
        {renderView()}
      </main>
    </div>
  );
}

export default function App() {
  const [history, setHistory] = useState<View[]>([View.FIELD_SELECTION]);
  const [currentViewIndex, setCurrentViewIndex] = useState(0);
  
  const [selectedCategory, setSelectedCategory] = useState<QuestionCategory | null>(null);
  const [selectedQuestion, setSelectedQuestion] = useState<Question | null>(null);
  const [questions, setQuestions] = useState(INITIAL_QUESTIONS);
  const [dontAskRedo, setDontAskRedo] = useState(false);

  const currentView = history[currentViewIndex];

  const navigateTo = (view: View) => {
    const newHistory = history.slice(0, currentViewIndex + 1);
    newHistory.push(view);
    setHistory(newHistory);
    setCurrentViewIndex(newHistory.length - 1);
  };

  const handleBack = () => {
    if (currentViewIndex > 0) {
      setCurrentViewIndex(prev => prev - 1);
    }
  };

  const handleForward = () => {
    if (currentViewIndex < history.length - 1) {
      setCurrentViewIndex(prev => prev + 1);
    }
  };

  const handleSelectCategory = (cat: QuestionCategory) => {
    setSelectedCategory(cat);
    if (cat === QuestionCategory.CUSTOM) {
      navigateTo(View.CUSTOM_DESCRIPTION);
    }
  };

  const handleStartPractice = () => {
    // Get all available questions for the selected category from the initial bank
    const allQuestions = INITIAL_QUESTIONS[selectedCategory!] || [];

    // Shuffle and pick 5 random questions
    const shuffled = [...allQuestions].sort(() => 0.5 - Math.random());
    const randomFive = shuffled.slice(0, 5);

    // Update the state to only show these 5 for the session
    setQuestions(prev => ({
      ...prev,
      [selectedCategory!]: randomFive
    }));

    if (randomFive.length > 0) {
      setSelectedQuestion(randomFive[0]);
      navigateTo(View.QUESTION_FLOW);
    } else {
       // Fallback or empty state
       navigateTo(View.ALL_QUESTIONS);
    }
  };

  const handleStartCustomJob = (description: string, generatedQuestions: string[]) => {
    // Convert generated strings to Question objects
    const newQuestions: Question[] = generatedQuestions.map((text, index) => ({
      id: `gen-${Date.now()}-${index}`,
      text: text,
      category: QuestionCategory.CUSTOM,
      type: 'Custom question'
    }));

    setQuestions(prev => ({
      ...prev,
      [QuestionCategory.CUSTOM]: [...(prev[QuestionCategory.CUSTOM] || []), ...newQuestions]
    }));
    
    // As per requirement "Display the list of questions that were generated earlier",
    // when "See all generated questions" is clicked in CustomJobInput, we navigate here.
    setSelectedCategory(QuestionCategory.CUSTOM);
    navigateTo(View.ALL_QUESTIONS);
  };

  const handleManualAddCustom = () => {
    navigateTo(View.CUSTOM_ADD);
  };

  const handleAddCustomQuestion = (text: string) => {
    const newQuestion: Question = {
      id: Date.now().toString(),
      text: text,
      category: QuestionCategory.CUSTOM,
      type: 'Custom question'
    };
    
    setQuestions(prev => {
      const updated = {
          ...prev,
          [QuestionCategory.CUSTOM]: [...(prev[QuestionCategory.CUSTOM] || []), newQuestion]
      };
      return updated;
    });

    setSelectedCategory(QuestionCategory.CUSTOM); 
    navigateTo(View.ALL_QUESTIONS);
  };

  const handleSeeAllQuestions = () => {
    // If the user wants to see all questions, we should ensure the full list is loaded
    // instead of the potential subset from "Practice with 5"
    if (selectedCategory && selectedCategory !== QuestionCategory.CUSTOM) {
       setQuestions(prev => ({
          ...prev,
          [selectedCategory]: INITIAL_QUESTIONS[selectedCategory]
       }));
    }
    navigateTo(View.ALL_QUESTIONS);
  };

  const handleSelectQuestion = (q: Question) => {
    setSelectedQuestion(q);
    navigateTo(View.QUESTION_FLOW);
  };

  const renderView = () => {
    switch (currentView) {
      case View.FIELD_SELECTION:
        return (
          <FieldSelection 
            selectedCategory={selectedCategory}
            onSelectCategory={handleSelectCategory}
            onStartPractice={handleStartPractice}
            onSeeAllQuestions={handleSeeAllQuestions}
          />
        );
      case View.CUSTOM_DESCRIPTION:
        return (
          <CustomJobInput 
            onStart={handleStartCustomJob}
            onManualAdd={handleManualAddCustom}
          />
        );
      case View.CUSTOM_ADD:
        return (
          <CustomQuestionInput 
             onAdd={handleAddCustomQuestion}
          />
        );
      case View.ALL_QUESTIONS:
        return (
          <Practice 
            category={selectedCategory || QuestionCategory.UX_DESIGN}
            questions={questions[selectedCategory!] || questions[QuestionCategory.UX_DESIGN]}
            onSelectQuestion={handleSelectQuestion}
            onAddCustomQuestion={handleManualAddCustom}
          />
        );
      case View.QUESTION_FLOW:
        return (
          <QuestionFlow 
            question={selectedQuestion!}
            onComplete={(rec) => console.log(rec)}
            dontAskRedo={dontAskRedo}
            setDontAskRedo={setDontAskRedo}
          />
        );
      default:
        return <div>Unknown View</div>;
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen text-slate-900 flex flex-col">
      <Navbar 
        onBack={handleBack} 
        onForward={handleForward}
        canGoBack={currentViewIndex > 0}
        canGoForward={currentViewIndex < history.length - 1}
      />
      <main className="flex-grow">
        {renderView()}
      </main>
    </div>
  );
}

payload.text:
In “+ Add Custom Question,” remove the smaller + icon and keep only the large + icon.

In the Voice option across the design, under “Recording your question,” add a pause function that allows the user to pause and resume the recording.

In the Camera option, add an automatic smooth skin filter to the functionality.

Set the back and forward arrow buttons to have a corner radius of 20

**Considering Interface Tweaks**

I'm currently focused on refining the user interface. Specifically, I'm examining the design of the "+ Add Custom Question" button within `components/Practice.tsx`. I believe the small plus icon might be redundant and could be removed for a cleaner, more streamlined look.


**Eliminating Redundancy**

I've been working on simplifying the "+ Add custom question" button design. The current implementation uses both a plus icon component and a "+" symbol within the text. Following feedback, I'm now exploring the removal of the icon component for a cleaner, less cluttered appearance. The focus is to keep a single, impactful "+" symbol.


**Simplifying the "+" Button**

I've been analyzing the design of the "+ Add custom question" button in `Practice.tsx` to refine its visual impact. The current implementation uses both a plus icon and a "+". The goal is to declutter by removing the icon. I'm focusing on the single, larger "+" symbol within the text for clarity. I'm now exploring how the button appears without the icon and reviewing potential size adjustments to enhance the design.


**Developing Pause/Resume Feature**

I'm now implementing the pause/resume functionality for voice recording. In `QuestionFlow.tsx`, under `RECORDING_VOICE`, I've integrated `MediaRecorder`'s `pause()` and `resume()` methods. This involved adding a `isPaused` state and incorporating a pause/resume button next to the visualizer, enhancing user control during voice recording.


**Adding a Beauty Filter**

I'm now integrating a basic "beauty filter" to enhance the camera feed. To achieve skin smoothing without heavy libraries, I've opted for a CSS filter. I'm applying `contrast(1.05) brightness(1.1) saturate(1.05)` to the `<video>` element within `renderView`. This creates a flattering lighting effect. I considered a slight drop shadow or blur, but ultimately discarded those for blurring details. This approach maintains a real-time, lightweight solution.


**Adjusting Video Presentation**

I'm currently working to refine the appearance of the camera feed. To enhance the visuals, I'm now integrating a CSS filter, specifically utilizing `brightness(1.05) contrast(1.02) saturate(1.05) sepia(0.03)`. This subtle filter creates a warmer, smoother look. I explored using a transparent overlay and `backdrop-filter`, but ultimately decided against them as they would impact elements behind the video. As an alternative, I considered a small blur, but decided against that as well. The ultimate goal is a lightweight, real-time "flattering" effect.

I'm also adjusting the corner radius of the back and forward arrow buttons in `components/Navbar.tsx`. Initially set to `rounded-full` (9999px), I'm now implementing a corner radius of 20px, specifically `rounded-[20px]` in Tailwind.


**Incorporating Pause Functionality**

I've been working on integrating pause/resume functionality within the voice recording view. My latest focus is on the `QuestionFlow.tsx` file, specifically the `RECORDING_VOICE` view. I've added a `isPaused` state variable to manage the pause/resume state. Based on this state, I have integrated the `MediaRecorder`'s `pause()` and `resume()` methods. This will allow the user to have greater control over their recordings. A pause/resume button has also been integrated next to the visualizer for seamless user interaction. I've also updated the timer logic to stop/start based on the `isPaused` state.


**Applying Cosmetic Touches**

I'm now integrating a CSS blur within the "beauty filter" in `renderView`, specifically adding `blur(0.4px)` to the existing filter. This minor blur will gently soften the skin texture, enhancing the overall appearance. Meanwhile, I'm updating the corner radius of back/forward buttons from `rounded-full` to `rounded-[20px]` in `components/Navbar.tsx` to give a less abrupt look. I've also implemented the removal of the small plus icon within the custom question button.
